40437.name: Runecarver's Grotto
40437.description: In the depths of the cavern, the Runecarver's Grotto breathes the art of ancestral craft. The air is filled with the scent of different woods and resins, each part of the sacred act of totem creation. Shelves lined with tools and ancient artifacts flank the walls, illuminated by a soft, otherworldly glow from runes etched into the stone. The gentle sound of chisels against wood echoes, as if in rhythm with the heartbeat of the earth itself.

40037.name: Cliff Steps
40037.description: The steps are wide and even, winding back and forth along the cliff face in tight zigzags. A railing offers some safety from the heights, for the fall is quite considerable. The stairway climbs the cliff heights toward the city and descends down to the beach several hundred feet below.

d.to_zone: Western Mareldja Coast - City of Mareldja

sentinel before a cave.
winding back and forth along the cliff face in

40086.name: City Dump
40086.description: Piles of scorched junk rise about you, smelling of sewage, ashes, and burning metal. The walls of junk form a circle through which you can view the sky. Underfoot is a rusted grate, which you could probably pry open with little effort.

d.to_room_name: The Quadruple Junction Under the Dump

40183.name: Beneath the Temple
40183.description: A palpable silence hangs low in the room, blocking out sound to those sleeping on the nearby orange cots. A table is set up with some simple supplies free to the weary traveller. A wooden doorway leads west.

40190.name: Beneath the Temple
40190.description: Sconced torches, mounted equidistant upon the curving stone walls, light a low, wide chamber beneath the temple plaza. An overwhelming scent drifts through the air, of musk and spice. Several squarish patterns of similar design are etched into the floor, bordered by rows of tiny candles. Stairs lead up and out of the chamber.

40191.name: A Smoky Alcove
40191.description: Dark colored fabric hung from wall to wall all but devours the meager light coming from hundreds of candles placed about the room. Central to the room is an enormous black gemstone gripped firmly by a skeletal hand anchored to a pedestal of femur bones. A carved door to the north holds the way out.

40411.name: Storage Room
40411.description: Wooden shelves line the walls, burdened with heavy crates marked and sealed to denote their contents; poison, knives, lock picks and other tools of the rogue's trade. Various miscellaneous clutter also litters the floor and a few of the tables.

40435.name: The Elemental Threshold
40435.description: The tapestry of nature's forces weave together here in a delicate balance. Veins of shimmering minerals embedded within the cavern walls emit a soft radiance, creating an interplay of light and shadow. Here, the essence of the ocean's breath and the heartbeat of the earth mingle, suffusing the air with a symphony of natural sounds and scents. Mist hovers over the ground, and the whispering echo of the sea's waves feels like the world itself is speaking in hushed tones.

40436.name: The Spirit Nexus
40436.description: A sacred chamber, enshrined within the heart of the cavern, is veiled in the dim glow of bioluminescent fungi and mineral formations that cast an ethereal light. Here, the walls are inscribed with deep, primal runes that seem to pulse with the shamanic energies that course through the earth's veins. The air is thick with the power of the elements, each breath a mixture of damp earth and the electric charge of a storm held at bay. In this secluded refuge, the whispers of ancestral spirits resonate, enveloping Shamans in the embrace of ages past.

40438.name: The Enchanting Crypt
40438.description: Veiled in perpetual twilight, this ancient crypt serves as a sanctum for necromantic enchantment. The air is thick with a chilling silence, broken only by the occasional drip of unseen moisture. Stone slabs line the walls, each carved with esoteric symbols and faintly glowing with necrotic energy. A narrow pathway leads back to the musty alcove to the north, while an archway to the east shrouds a deeper darkness within. Here, the boundary between the living and the dead seems to blur, as if inviting the curious to delve into the forbidden arts of disenchantment.

40000.name: Central Plaza
40000.description: You stand in the center of a spacious plaza, its periphery adorned with potted plants and carved stone benches. People stroll about you, clad in bright silks and chatting amongst themselves. A bronze seal at your feet declares you to be in Mareldja, Crown on the Water. A breeze tinged with salt and brine blows eastward, and shorebirds wheel and dive gracefully overhead. Four wide streets lead from the plaza at each of the compass points.

40001.name: Fumbles` Fixit Shoppe
40001.description: Bits of wire and pieces of leather and iron are scattered over the surface of the counter in front of you. Tools hang on pegs along the back wall, each of them showing much use but also loving care. There is a sign hanging on the east wall.

40002.name: Dump Area
40002.description: The alley ends in a large cul-de-sac amidst piles of junk. A hole in the cobblestones looks as if it has acted as the city waste basket for some time. The alley leads back west.

40003.name: Garden
40003.description: A cobbled path leads through a small park north of the city. Trees on either side of you rustle in the breeze, and beds of flowers supply bursts of color to the greenness of the grass. A low stone wall encircles the area to the north, and a crossroads to the south lead around and into the city. A small wooden sign sprouts from a nearby bed of flowers.

and beds of flowers supply bursts of

40004.name: By the Cliff
40004.description: The cliff drops steeply away, presenting a dazzling view of the beach below. A low stone wall has crumbled along the border of the cliff, and gliding shorebirds have nested in the ruins. The sky overhead is a vast cobalt, vivid and endless. A stiff breeze blows inland, and a forest of hills and valleys carpets the land to the east.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest

40005.name: Outside the City
40005.description: You find yourself outside the Gate of Fire, at the edge of a small park. A path winds through the grass to the edge of the cliff to the north, and a well- worn road travels east and west around the outer walls. The Gate of Fire lies to the south, allowing passage into Mareldja.

40006.name: The Gate of Fire
40006.description: The Gate of Fire sits astride the Concourse of Fire and allows passage through Mareldja's northern wall. It is a simple affair of wood bound in iron braces. Tiles glazed with reds and oranges adorn the archway. The Concourse leads north into the city cemetery and south toward the central plaza.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: Braces.  Tiles glazed with reds and oranges adorn the archway.  The Concourse
Gate of Fire

Through Mareldja's northern wall.  It is a simple affair of wood bound in iron

40007.name: The Wheel of Bread
40007.description: The smell of baking bread makes your mouth water. A counter displays several types, ranging from small loaves to wheels of rye and sourdough. Flour seems to coat everything like dust.

40008.name: Pet Shop
40008.description: Squawks and yowls fill this shop. Cages surround you containing animals both exotic and mundane. A sign above the counter says in crude lettering:

40010.name: Concourse of Fire
40010.description: Trees bearing bright blue blossoms alternated with globes atop carved iron poles line the Concourse of Fire. The way extends north toward the gate and south toward a large open plaza. Gaily colored flowers in window boxes decorate houses built from great blocks of dusty beige stone.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: City guard arrives in from the south.

40011.name: The Glittering Eye
40011.description: Expensive glass cases show off gems of every cut and color. A fist-sized diamond gleams in the center of the largest, clearly the "eye" of the shop. Globes of light on the ends of polished brass rods illuminate the room almost as brightly as daylight. A thick carpet of purple and grey nestles against your feet, and the faint smell of incense teases your nostrils.

65100.name: The Mareldjan Press Office
65100.description: The spacious expansion is currently undergoing hurried construction for an office to develop news print. A foreman oversees the work effort while several laborers construct shelves and wooden framework to support heavy equipment. South leads to the archives of the Library.

40014.name: At Eternity and Sorrows
40014.description: The Avenue of Eternity meets with the Street of Sorrows to form an elbow bending east and south. A stray tabby cat yowls beseechingly by your feet, and an ocean breeze sighs against your face. A stone well sits in the street center, carved with whorls and fish.

40015.name: Avenue of Eternity
40015.description: A wooden sign painted with a lemniscate symbol indicates that you travel The Avenue of Eternity. Children run past you, laughing, and a dog noses its way through the gutter. The Avenue passes through a market to the east and ends further to the west. A shop with a dragonet painted on a sign stands to the north. Crude lettering above it spells out "Pet Shop".

40016.name: Market Square
40016.description: The marketplace is a spacious plaza dotted chaotically with multi-colored tents. Cast iron poles topped with small globes of glassy material rise up at intervals around the perimeter. Merchants under striped awnings and beside blankets laden with wares implore you to consider their goods. The Concourse of Fire bisects the Avenue of Eternity, the former running north and south and the latter east and west.

40017.name: Avenue of Eternity
40017.description: The Avenue of Eternity is cobbled with the same beige stone that comprises the buildings and walls. Gutters on either side allow water to be washed through rusted iron grates. A small shop with a sign depicting a large eye with a diamond glittering in the center stands to the north, and the avenue continues east and west.

leather drinking flask

40018.name: At Eternity and Painters
40018.description: The Avenue of Eternity connects with the Painters' Boulevard here in this northeastern corner of the city, the former leading west and the latter leading south. A single purple flame dances in the window of a shop to the east, and above it, the cryptic symbol of the Mage's Guild. Above you hang garments on ropes stretched across from window to window.

40019.name: The Purple Flame
40019.description: The Purple Flame is owned and operated by the Guild of Mages. All manner of magical potions, wands, scrolls, rods and other impedimenta fill the walls around you. The smell of incense mingles with that of drying herbs and alchemical supplies. A large, disembodied eye floats about the shop, deterring thievery. Past the counter to the east is a little curtain leading further into the guild. The doorway is to the west.

40020.name: The Chamber of Magic
40020.description: Bookshelves stuffed with books and arcane scrolls line the walls. In the corner is a desk piled high with inventory lists and mysterious curios. A globe suspended from the center of the room bathes the chamber in an eternal glow. A curtain to the west leads out to the shop area, and a stairway leads up to the second floor.

40022.name: Street of Sorrows
40022.description: The Street of Sorrows stretches out to the south and ends a little way to the north. To the west is a small shop with a picture of an open trunk on the sign before it. Brightly clad people pass you, heading north or south along the thoroughfare.

40023.name: Temple Plaza
40023.description: The temple plaza is paved with red and brown cobblestones, all laid out in an intricate pattern. The plaza stretches magnificently to the north. Off to the west are the priests' and clerics' quarters. The Concourse of Fire lies to the east and the Concourse of Water to the south.

40024.name: Concourse of Fire
40024.description: The concourse runs north and ends to the south at a central plaza. On either side rise stately buildings. To the east is the library, and to the west is the temple. Through the pillars you can see something gleaming.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: Concourse of Fire

Stray dog looks at you expectantly.

and to the

40025.name: Library Steps
40025.description: The steps to the library are wide and shallow, containing bits of glass which sparkle in the light. The library is a plain structure, built of massive stone blocks that look as if they will withstand the ages. The steps lead down to the west and south, and a large wooden door to the east leads into the library.

40026.name: Painters Boulevard
40026.description: Graceful purple and pink flowered trees line the street, and dusty stone buildings sport ledges of flowers and hanging pots. A few pigeons flap noisily away as you interrupt their search for food. The street continues north and intersects with the Concourse of Earth to the south.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest

Painters Boulevard
and dusty stone

40036.name: Borash`s General Supplies
40036.description: Odds and ends are scattered chaotically on tables and benches. You see lengths of rope, barrels, trunks, bags and torches all mixed together in helpless piles. It seems that at one time someone made an effort to sort them onto their proper tables, but now everything is in disarray.

40038.name: Landing
40038.description: A wide landing sits at the top of a stairway heading down the cliff. Iron railings allow you to lean out and gaze at the sea, which glitters a deep turquoise far below. White shorebirds ride the currents above you, and you can hear the distant crash of the waves. The Gate of Water allows passage east into the city, and a road heads north around the outer walls.

40039.name: The Gate of Water
40039.description: Ceramic tiles decorated with watery designs line the archway of this tiny gate. Out through the opening, you can see a landing at the edge of the cliff, and beyond it, the open sea. The Concourse of Water stretches out to the east, heading further into the city.

40040.name: Concourse of Water
40040.description: Laughter spills out from a building to your south where a green tankard is painted onto the sign above. A scruffy dog pokes its way through some garbage in a nearby alley, and gulls float lazily overhead. The concourse heads east and ends at the Gate of Water to the west.

40041.name: At Water and Sorrows
40041.description: The Concourse of Water intersects with the Street of Sorrows, which runs from north to south. Upper level windows sport wooden boxes holding geraniums and snapdragons. Here and there a voice rings out in anger or in laughter, and people in bright silks pass you in the street. The concourse continues east and west.

40042.name: Concourse of Water
40042.description: The Concourse of Water bustles with pedestrian traffic to and from the palace to the south and the temple to the north. The concourse becomes a large plaza to the east and continues west toward the city gates. White pigeons wander underfoot, cooing and looking for crumbs.

40043.name: Concourse of Earth
40043.description: The Concourse of Earth runs from the center of the city to the eastern gate. To the north stands the library of Mareldja, filled with knowledge both old and new. To the south you see a park surrounded by an old stone wall and a wrought iron gate filled with birds and ivy.

40044.name: At Earth and Painters
40044.description: The Boulevard of Painters' stretches away north and south from you. Stately scarlet-leaved trees line the concourse, bearing a hard orange fruit. The concourse leads west toward the city and east toward the gates.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest

40045.name: Concourse of Earth
40045.description: Tall, stately trees line the concourse as it runs form the city center out To the north is a square building with an anchor attached to the outside. A sign hanging next to the door bears the picture of a wrecked ship.

n.to_room_name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity

stately trees line the concourse as it runs form the city center out

25198.name: Orca Sanctuary
25198.description: Deep beneath the ocean's surface, this sanctuary offers a serene and protected environment for orcas to thrive. The water here is illuminated by the soft glow of bioluminescent plants and corals. Scattered throughout the sanctuary are numerous underwater grottos and caves, providing orcas with safe spaces to retreat, rest, and raise their young.

40048.name: Common Area
40048.description: People seated at tables about the room fill the air with quiet conversation. A large fire on the northern wall cooks a chunk of meat as a servant turns the spit. The wooden floor is swept clear of debris, and the tables are conscientiously wiped down.

w.to_room_name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity

25199.name: Sparse Kelp Forests
25199.description: Patches of dense kelp forests sway gently with the currents which offer shelter and food for countless marine species. These underwater gardens are home to a myriad of creatures, from tiny invertebrates to larger predators, contributing to the rich biodiversity of the area. During certain seasons, these waters becomes a bustling highway for migrating whales and fish.

40050.name: Outside the City
40050.description: Outside the Gate of Earth, the road winds eastward and downward to disappear into the Forest of Jharet a quarter mile away. To the west, the city of Mareldja crowns the cliff top, inviting you to enter and stay a while.

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: Into the Forest of Jharet a quarter mile away.  To the west
Mareldja crowns the cliff top
Multitude of fireflies wander lazily.
Outside the City
Outside the Gate of Earth

the city of
the road winds eastward and downward to disappear

40051.name: At the Bar
40051.description: You find yourself crowded on both sides by unsavory looking characters. The bar consists of a few long planks covered with a dark, sticky layer. Odd looking bottles line the wall behind, some covered in dust but none of them very clean.

40052.name: The Crystal Altar
40052.description: Light refracts from the altar's facets and makes the whole thing seem to shimmer. Carved from a single slab, the altar is about four feet high and three feet on either side. Pale purple lilacs are spread upon it, offered by some unknown worshipper as a gift to the gods. After stepping down from the dais, east is your only exit.

40053.name: Priestly Compound
40053.description: The compound is spacious but sparse. Frescoes around the walls depict the gods and goddesses of the realm. A table near the entranceway holds several books of chants and prayers. Smoke from a cone of incense twirls lazily up toward the ceiling. The plaza lies to the east, and a small doorway, to the west, leads further into the compound.

40054.name: The Chamber of Stillness
40054.description: Books on every conceivable subject line the walls of this chamber, and an air of sanctity has settled like dust upon everything. Various holy vessels and symbols lie upon a rug, gleaming softly in the light. Spread out before the rug is a diagram of the universe.

40055.name: Sanctum Sanctorum
40055.description: Blue tinged flames flicker over a small altar in the center of the room. You do not smell any smoke. A calmness lies about this room, and about your soul.

40056.name: Library
40056.description: You are surrounded by books and scrolls, countless and diverse, of all shapes and subjects. The air in here has that dusty smell associated with books and paper. Light shines in from long, lancet windows. The exit lies to the west, and a doorway to the north leads to the archives.

40057.name: Archives
40057.description: This room contains the history of the realm. In the center is a rosewood table and several chairs. Various histories line the shelves, and delicate oil lamps give this room a cheery glow. A mural of Mareldja viewed from the sea decorates the northern wall. The city seems to gleam upon the cliff top. A doorway to the west leads to the map room and another leads south to the main part of the library. Build narrow and tight, a spiral staircase twists through a small hole in the ceiling.

40058.name: Map Room
40058.description: Maps adorn the walls and fill dusty scroll cases. You see a map of the city Mareldja, maps of the surrounding lands, and maps of lands of which you have never heard. Some are old and obviously outdated, but some are quite new.

40059.name: The Green Tankard
40059.description: The Green Tankard is a rather well-to-do tavern with good beer and a cheerful ambiance. People talking, drinking or gambling fill the tables. Shelves covered with different kinds of tankards line three walls, and a bar stands in the fourth.

40060.name: The Street of Sorrows
40060.description: The Street of Sorrows runs north and south. To the east is an old stone house, decayed and deserted. People pass you, singly or in pairs, going about their daily business. Bright silk whispers against dusty stone, and the smell of the sea is present in the air.

40061.name: The House of Illiar
40061.description: The house is dusty and deserted. A simple tapestry decorates the south. You wonder why no one has plundered the house yet. A sense of unease works its way down your spine. Stairs lead up to the second floor.

40065.name: Palace Gates
40065.description: The palace of Mareldja sits in the center of a well-trimmed lawn to the south. Gold glints off the central dome, and a banner of grey and deep turquoise floats from the highest tower. To the north is the Concourse of Water, which runs from the western gate to the city center.

s.to_zone: Royal Palace of Mareldja - City of Mareldja

w.to_zone: Mareldjan Guard Barracks - City of Mareldja

40066.name: Concourse of Air
40066.description: The Concourse of Air is a wide street lined with shady trees and globes atop carved iron poles. A palace rises to the west behind a fence, its design simple yet elegant. The concourse ends to the north at the central plaza and continues south toward the city gates. Central Plaza

40068.name: Stables
40068.description: The warm, organic smell of manure surrounds you in this open building, and your feet rustle against the straw and feathers littering the ground. Stalls containing different kinds of mounts extend out in rows.

40073.name: Painters Boulevard
40073.description: So named for the public frescoes adorning the walls of some of the buildings in this area, the Painters' Boulevard is a collective portfolio of the city's artists, both good and bad. A small shop lies to the east, its outside a mixture of ships and heraldic symbols. The boulevard continues north and south. At Dreams and Painters

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: Alert young guard patrols the city streets.
Artists
In this area

Painters Boulevard

the Painters' Boulevard is a collective portfolio of the city's

40074.name: Jansen`s Bargains
40074.description: This shop is low and cluttered with various items, from swords to shields to cloaks to shoes. Light from an open window highlights the dust on the tables. Bright bits of paper attached to glasses, rings, boots, and weapons scream out their prices. A sign over the counter says in large letters, "Jansen's Bargains: Best Prices in Town".

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: "Jansen's
Bargains:  Best Prices in Town".
Bright bits of paper attached to glasses
Cloaks to shoes.  Light from an open window highlights the dust on the tables.
Jansen squints at you speculatively.
Jansen`s Bargains

rings

40075.name: Farath`s Armory
40075.description: The walls are covered with shields of bronze, wood, and steel. In a corner hang an assortment of gauntlets, helmets, and bracers, and in another, a half- finished breastplate. From a back room, you can hear pounding. A window in the east wall allows you to look out onto the street.

40076.name: At Dreams and Sorrows
40076.description: The Avenue of Dreams meets with the Street of Sorrows, forming an elbow leading east and north. Brightly garbed inhabitants and exotic foreigners pass you under buildings of solid dusty stone. Laughter floats from a building to the south, and the sounds of a smithy to the west.

40077.name: Avenue of Dreams
40077.description: The Avenue of Dreams passes to the south of the palace. Through the tall, iron fence, you can watch the gardeners work on the grounds, trimming hedges and pulling weeds. A dome rises from the palace center, leafed in gold and shining in the light. The street runs east and west.

40078.name: At Dreams and Air
40078.description: You are at the intersection between the Avenue of Dreams and the Concourse of Air. Inhabitants of the city pass you, conversing with one another and walking singly. A dog slinks across your path, and a hammering can be heard from the south, now faint, now loud.

40079.name: Avenue of Dreams
40079.description: The Avenue meets with the Concourse of Air to the west and continues to the east. Trees with white blossoms shower petals down upon you with every breath of wind. The roofs in this section contain plants in large urns, and with a little help, you could probably climb up if you wanted. At Dreams and Air

40080.name: At Dreams and Painters
40080.description: The Painters' Boulevard, which leads north, meets with the Avenue of Dreams, which heads west. Pigeons flutter from an alley to the south, looking for food amongst some rubbish piles.

40081.name: The Steel Helm
40081.description: The bar is packed with fighter types smelling of sweat and steel and talking about the finer points of bash and parry. Tables bear the carved names of legends and all those who aspire to glory. The Steel Helm caters to members of the Guild of Fighters, providing a place to relax after practice sessions or catch up on the local gossip.

40082.name: Weapons Smith
40082.description: The shop is neat and clean. Daggers, knives, and smaller axes cover two tables near the entrance, and larger weapons are displayed upon the walls. A counter runs across the room to the west, and past it you can see a furnace and table scattered with tools.

40083.name: Concourse of Air
40083.description: People brush past you clad in bright, flowing silks. From the shop to the west comes the sound of metal striking metal. Smoke pours from a sooty chimney and a sign over the doorway shows a pair of crossed halberds. The Concourse of Air passes through the city gate to the south and continues north toward the central plaza. An affluent-looking building stands to the east.

40084.name: Narrow Alley
40084.description: Heaps of junk are piled against each wall, consisting of scraps of fabric, broken pottery, and smashed bottles. White pigeons flap overhead and coo from their nesting places. The alley ends at a street to the north and makes a bend to the east.

40085.name: Narrow Alley
40085.description: The alley runs between shabby and neglected buildings. Broken bottles and bits of nameless debris litter the street and clog the gutter. A plain building to the south sports a faded sign painted with the words, "Fenric's Surplus". The alley runs east and west. Fenric`s Surplus

40087.name: Practice Yard
40087.description: An assortment of weapons, real and practice, fill portable racks along the outside wall. The ground is packed earth, trodden smooth by hundreds of booted feet. A canopy of oilcloth supported by high beams keeps the rain and beating sun from those who would better their martial skills.

40088.name: The Gate of Air
40088.description: The Gate of Air allows traffic to and from the southern part of Mareldja. painted with light blue and white whorls decorate the stone around the arch.

40089.name: Fenric`s Surplus
40089.description: Fenric has his wares laid out on four wobbly tables, sorted by type. They look second hand, but serviceable for the most part. Flies buzz lazily overhead and a mangy dog lies in a corner. A doorway to the west leads to a back room, and another leads north to an alley.

40090.name: Back Room
40090.description: Acting as a supply room for the Guild of Thieves, this room has a little of everything. Crates and boxes are stacked haphazardly along the walls. An assortment of hats lies in the corner, and a tray of disguising cosmetics sits balanced upon a box marked "pocketed garments". A curtained doorway stands to the south and another leads east to the shop.

40091.name: Instruction Room
40091.description: Various keys, picks, and practice locks lie atop a table positioned along the far wall, looking oiled and well used. A chest with a false bottom sits dismantled by the doorway, and lead-filled coins lie in a pile beside it. Light from a magical globe shines down upon you, illuminating everything in a quiet light.

40092.name: Outside the City
40092.description: The Concourse of Air exits the city to the north and winds its way down to a cluster of buildings next to the harbor. Gulls drift back and forth on stiff wings, and the tang of brine is strong in the air. On either side of you rise the mansions of the noble and powerful of Mareldja, surrounded by high iron fences.

40093.name: Concourse of Air
40093.description: You follow the Concourse of Air as it travels north toward the city or west down to the harbor. Houses and mansions lie on either side of the road, though you can see that they get smaller as their distance from the city increases.

40094.name: Concourse of Air
40094.description: The city of Mareldja sits like a crown atop the cliff. A stiff breeze blows inland, smelling of salt and fish. Down on the harbor you can see the sails of merchant ships and pleasure craft. The Concourse of Air slopes steeply down to the docks and climbs up to the city proper. A seedy looking tavern sits to the south, and you see, hear, and smell stables to the north.

40095.name: Harbor
40095.description: Waves lap against the wooden quay, flecks of foam bobbing up and down. Ships rock gently in the water, and gulls scream overhead. To the north is the harbormaster's office. The Concourse of Air climbs up the cliff to the southern gate of Mareldja.

40096.name: The Harbormaster`s Office
40096.description: The office is decorated with functional nautical equipment and maps of every coastline imaginable. From the neatness of the office and its contents, you get the feeling that the harbormaster was once a captain of his own ship.

40097.name: The Cup and Tippler
40097.description: A cracked picture of a merry drunk sitting astride a barrel of ale hangs above the bar. Tables are close and crowded, and not very clean. The clientele is a rough lot consisting of sailors and local toughs. Several eye you speculatively.

40099.name: Citybank
40099.description: The name inspires faith and trust in you, and you know that your savings will be safe here. The atmosphere of the room is cozy and self- satisfied. Well dressed tellers stand behind polished marble counters and await your business. The brass in the room bears no fingerprints.

40157.name: The Outer Road
40157.description: The road runs around the city perimeter, heading east toward the northern gate and south along the western wall. The grass on the cliff is a deep and brilliant green, filled with small yellow and blue flowers.

40158.name: The Outer Road
40158.description: The dusty beige wall of Mareldja rises to the east, and the cliff to the west ends about ten feet past the road. White shorebirds sweep above you, and the surf provides a continuous susurration in the background. The road continues north and south.

40159.name: The Outer Road
40159.description: The road follows a strip of land between the city wall and the cliff edge. An iron railing about ten feet to the west overlooks a long drop down to a narrow beach below. The road follows the wall perimeter to the north and ends at a landing to the south.

40160.name: The Outer Road
40160.description: The walls of Mareldja are built from great blocks of beige stone, fitted together so precisely that the mortar never exceeds a half an inch in width. You think that it might even be unnecessary. The road continues to the west and south, following the perimeter of the city. Another road, paved with somber, black stones heads off to the east.

40161.name: The Outer Road
40161.description: Wide enough for two carts to pass each other, the road along the perimeter of Mareldja is hard and packed, filled with clay and tiny pebbles. The road runs north and south. To the east, you see the land carpeted in green as the cliff falls away to the Forest of Jharet.

40162.name: The Outer Road
40162.description: Directly to the south, the road intersects with the Concourse of Earth as it Looking to the north, you can see how the cliff eventually ends and the land meets a blue, blue sky.

40163.name: Road of Tears
40163.description: Sand grits under your feet as you move, making a slight grinding noise. In the distance to the north-east, you can see mountains raising their majestic heads against the sky. To your west, you can see the Outer Road running along the wall of Mareldja. The Road of Tears continues east. A path leads off through the grasses to the north.

40164.name: Road of Tears
40164.description: The voices of insects and birds are strangely absent here, as if they too share the somber mood of this ebony road. The walls of Mareldja can be seen in the distance to the west and the road continues to the east.

40165.name: Road of Tears
40165.description: Grasslands stretch away to the south, moving rhythmically under the hand of a mild breeze. A large, black gate intersects the road to the north and the Road of Tears continues west.

40167.name: Weedy Track
40167.description: Unkempt weeds reach as high as your waist as you move along this rarely traveled road. Mareldja's outer wall bounds you on the west and looms high above your head. To the north you can see the eastern city gate while southward, the trail disappears into the choking vegetation.

40168.name: Weedy Track
40168.description: The weeds and brush are so thick that the trail you are following seems to be mostly imaginary. The imaginary trail seems a little more passable to the north and the east.

40169.name: Dense Thicket
40169.description: You have come upon a thicket, densely populated with young birch trees. A few large oaks and elms block out parts of the sky, but that is only at the edges of the thicket. A weed-choked trail leads to the west and to the east is what looks to be a small clearing.

40170.name: Clearing
40170.description: A weather-worn, clapboard shack to the east makes an angular eyesore against the natural beauty of this small clearing. A small curl of smoke from the chimney indicates that the shack has not been abandoned and its door stands invitingly open. A stand of young birch stands to the west and a narrow trail leads south.

40171.name: Clapboard Shack
40171.description: A long counter runs the length of the room, its surface piled high with tanned furs and rough clothing. There is a pleasant smell in the air, one that reminds you of soft beds and warm fires on cold nights.

40172.name: Worn Trail
40172.description: You move along a well-worn trail running downslope toward a large forest to the east and up toward a large city to the west. The footing is even and the travel easy. Worn Trail

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: East and up toward a large city to the west.  The footing is even and the
Travel easy.
Worn Trail
You move along a well-worn trail running downslope toward a large forest to

40173.name: Worn Trail
40173.description: The forest bulks large and gloomy to the east. Strange cries and soft, whispering moans occasionally come faintly from the wooded depths. The passage of your feet raises small clouds of dust from the road's packed and even surface. The road travels east to west. The Edge of the Forest

chests.name: Iron and wooden chest
contents: Forest bulks large and gloomy to the east.  Strange cries and soft
Of your feet raises small clouds of dust from the road's packed and even
Surface.  The road travels east to west.
Whispering moans occasionally come faintly from the wooded depths.  The passage
Worn Trail

40175.name: Forest Road
40175.description: The forest bulks large and gloomy to the east. Strange cries and soft, whispering moans occasionally come faintly from the wooded depths. The passage of your feet raises small clouds of dust from the road's packed and even surface. The road continues to the north and south. Forest Road

40176.name: Forest Road
40176.description: The sounds of furtive movement seem to pace you, just out of sight behind the trees. Fortunately the road only skirts along the edge of the forest and doesn't actually go through it. Forest Road

40177.name: Forest Road
40177.description: The trail rises to the south as it climbs a small rocky hill. This way is a well trafficked road, however, so travel is easy. The road continues straight to the north. Hillock

40178.name: Hillock
40178.description: The path makes a sharp bend here, heading downslope as it moves north. To the east, the trail goes only a short distance before it turns south again.

40179.name: Rocky Slope
40179.description: A short, steep path leads down to join a pleasant country road to the south. A rock-strewn trail heads west for a short distance before it turns to the north. Country Road

40181.name: Inside the Bottle
40181.description: The light shining in through the crimson glass washes everything in a bloody glow. Through thick, smooth walls, you can see the contents of a giant shop. Far far above your head the sides of the bottle narrow to hold a cork. You tap and tap on the glass, but you really have no hope of breaking it. No exits are apparent.

40185.name: Temple Plaza
40185.description: A winding cobblestone path meanders past sprawling gardens and bubbling fountains, terminating near an expanse of brightly colored tile, where a worn stretch of thick carpeting descends down a series of marble steps into an incensed basement. To the north, a magnificent black marble building rises majestically, its intricate details and striking dome catching the eye. Ceramic tiled steps lead west to the temple altar and you can see something gleaming from within. The temple plaza leads back out to the south.

40188.name: Musty Alcove
40188.description: Ancient dust hangs in the air perpetually within this tiny alcove, tucked away in a little-explored corner of the city. Musty tomes have been piled all over the room, and it doesn't look like anyone has read them for centuries. A few faint chants can be heard from through an open doorway to the east, and the relatively fresh air of the alley lies to the south.

40189.name: Chamber of Bones
40189.description: This stone chamber is dominated by a large obsidian dias that lies at its center, covered with etched runes of wicked import. A massive chandelier of intricately carved bone dangles from the ceiling, providing just enough flickering candlelight to permit study of any necromantic texts you might wish to peruse during your stay in this dank room of death. A rickety blackened sign hangs from a wall.

40046.name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity
40046.description: The inside of this inn is decorated to resemble the inside of a ship. Nautical instruments and seascapes hang from the wall, and a much battered figurehead rests in a corner, a plaque above gleaming of polished brass. A wooden staircase leads up to the second floor, and a doorway leading west heads into a bright red room out of which come waves of heated air. More hospitable options lie east into the common room, or outside toward one of the city concourses. GMCPDebug: off Concourse of Earth

40049.name: The Gate of Earth
40049.description: Tiles glazed with earthy tones outline the gateway. Through it, the concourse enters the city to the west or heads out into the forest to the east. The forest covers the cliff as it drops, and looking out, you can see for a very long way.

contents: Concourse enters the city to the west or heads out into the forest to the east.
Forest covers the cliff as it drops
Gate of Earth
Gruff looking mercenary eyes you with distrust as he guards his charge.
Huge pile of free kindling has been heaped up here.
Merchant bustles by.

you can see for a

40299.name: The Red Room
40299.description: Deep crimson walls surrounding and supporting a vermilion floor and ceiling, coupled with an intense, but just barely tolerable heat, welcome you as you step inside. The room is rather bare, and seems to exist only to display a somewhat dubious looking bulletin board which stands at the center. It appears to have postings lining both faces, and has obviously suffered badly in this environment. Most of the exposed wood is blackened, nearly charred, though the various notes seem unaffected, despite being fully aflame....

e.to_room_name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity

10100.name: Reception
10100.description: Polished brass lanterns give this room a cheery glow. A desk rests against the eastern wall, covered with books and accounts, and a large picture on the opposite wall affords a view of a wrecked ship lying on a beach, its hull smashed in. An open window behind the desk allows the sounds of the street to filter up from below.

d.to_room_name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity

93097.name: Along the Quay
93097.description: Gulls put forth minimum effort to flutter out of your way as you walk down the wooden walkway of the quay. A few smaller fishing vessels bob gently, their crews pulling filled nets ashore. The dock could accomodate larger ships, but few seem to make the journey from distant lands.

s.to_room_name: The Southern Extent of Mareldja Harbor

93101.name: The Cantilevered Launch
93101.description: An open field has had its grass cut, or trampled, and presses to the edge of the ragged cliff's edge. A strange wooden boardwalk begins nearby and runs directly to the edge 50 feet away, and continues smoothly out into free space another 30 feet with a rough truss supporting it from below.

40408.name: The Steel Helm
40408.description: The polished wooden surface of the bar gleams in the flickering light of several iron lanterns, which hang around the room. The air smells of sweat, leather, ale and whatever the kitchens prepared for today as the sounds of merriment and conversation surround you.

40409.name: The Helm's Common Room
40409.description: Heavy oaken tables lay scattered about the room surrounded by chairs and benches, all facing the direction of an elevated stage on the eastern side. Various weapons and shields decorate the walls and groups of adventurers sit around tables chatting.

40410.name: Study Room
40410.description: Plush chairs surround little wooden reading tables scattered around the room, which are piled high with books and scrolls and whatever else the guild magicians choose to study. Mage lights float around the room to provide ample illumination for those reading, while offering zero risk of fire. A large, runed stone sits in the center of the room.

40417.name: The Chamber of Reflection
40417.description: Altars to every god are present here, save one for Thurvun. Priests and clerics mill around each, caring for them and seeing if any of those present require anything. A feeling of peace surrounds you as you take in the serenity of the room.

40419.name: The Matchmaker's Shop
40419.description: Large windows high in the wall let in a lot of natural light, brightening the space and complimenting an array of various flowers and potted plants scattered around the room. A large, brightly colored carpet covers the floor and paintings of different couples cover the walls.

40420.name: The Enchanter's Study
40420.description: Dazzling lights hang around the room, bobbing gently in place. Rows upon rows of shelves line the walls, weighed down by countless objects of mystical importance. Robed students sit around and focus on various mundane items as they attempt to hone their enchantments under the watchful eye of an experienced guild member.

40423.name: Deep Inside the Archives
40423.description: The walls are lined with large mahogany bookcases that extend all the way up to the vaulted ceiling. An iron chandelier hangs from the center burning 12 lavender candles incased in glass bulbs. Each individual shelf is labeled in an alphabetical order and a large sliding ladder would allow you to scale around the bookcases easily. A portion of one of the bookcases, roughly the size of a doorway, is slightly ajar.

40424.name: A Cozy Study
40424.description: Several desks are placed along the sides of the room. Each one has a standing divider between them and is accompanied with plush chairs, a small bin to place books and an unlit lantern. The center of the room hosts several curved couches which incircle a round mahogany table. A long bookcase stands at the back wall of the room and is labeled: "Known Inhabitants of the Land. "

40425.name: Inside the Coastal Watchtower
40425.description: The base of the watchtower perfectly portrays the primary function of its construction: Efficiency. A large map can be seen against the south wall of the watchtower littered with several pins and markings. A simple wooden staircase offers a way to ascend the watchtower, and the vestibule extends to the north. The door exits out to the Concourse of Water's massive gate.

40426.name: Coastal Watchtower Lobby
40426.description: A massive table stands in the center of the room absent of chairs. It appears this extent of the building was designed for the sole purpose of brief collaboration between officials and dispatching. The walls are bare, devoid of any windows or notable decor. A couple shelves against the back wall are stacked with stationery, ink pens and a variety of other mundane accessories. The lobby extends south to a massive doorway and a wooden staircase.

40427.name: Coastal Watchtower Staircase Landing
40427.description: The staircase ascends to a landing surrounded by windows, or descends to the base of the tower that offers an exit to the Concourse. Only a couple torch sconces are placed along the wall next to the landing. A large window overlooks the city to the east.

40428.name: Coastal Watchtower Lookout
40428.description: The landing is mostly bare, aside from the windows that line the entire perimeter. With the city far below, the tower offers a beautifully strategic vantage point. The vast ocean swells in from the west, the dense Jharet forest extends well beyond the city to the east, snowcapped mountains stand tall beyond grassy hills to the north and the country road is visible leading south to Oldgate in the distance alongside the Brown River Valley. The tower descends by the way of a wooden staircase in the center of the landing.

40430.name: Green Tankard's Kitchen
40430.description: The kitchen is a hot, cramped space filled with the smells of cooking food and the sound of sizzling pans. The air is thick with smoke and the floor is slick with grease. A large hearth dominates one wall, and various pots, pans, and utensils hang from the ceiling and walls.

40431.name: The Game Room
40431.description: The game room is a small, dingy space filled with tables and games. There are tables for cards, dice, and other games, as well as a billiards table and a dartboard. The room is filled with the sounds of laughter and curses, and the air is thick with the smells of tobacco and alcohol. Patrons can gamble or watch others play while enjoying a cheap drink. The atmosphere is rough and the stakes can be high.

40432.name: The Green Tankard
40432.description: The common room is always filled with a mix of locals and travelers, who come to come to enjoy the friendly atmosphere, good food, and cold ale that the Green Tankard is known for. The room is often lively, with patrons chatting and laughing with each other, or engaging in games of chance or skill.

40434.name: Before the Mausoleum
40434.description: The entrance to the mausoleum is grand and imposing, showcasing the intricate black marble that adorns its walls. A soaring dome stands at its center, surrounded by delicate stained glass windows that filter soft light into the room. The floor is made of the same ceramic tile as the rest of the temple structure. At the far end of the room, two large doors made of the same black marble as the walls stand ready to admit visitors to the mausoleum.

40021.name: Inner Sanctum
40021.description: The floor is quite bare and bereft of decoration. Scorch marks on the walls attest silently to the power of the spells released here in the past. But, you wonder, toward what end? The room has no windows and is lit from no discernible light source.

40047.name: Reception
40047.description: Polished brass lanterns give this room a cheery glow. A desk rests against the eastern wall, covered with books and accounts, and a large picture on the opposite wall affords a view of a wrecked ship lying on a beach, its hull smashed in. An open window behind the desk allows the sounds of the street to filter up from below.

d.to_room_name: The Inn of the Golden Vanity

40062.name: Hallway
40062.description: An old woven carpet of green and blue follows the hallway as it leads north to a study and south to a bedroom. A sculpture of a garlanded woman sits at the top of a staircase. You notice that the garland she wears is real.

40063.name: Study
40063.description: A large walnut desk sits against the wall facing the doorway next to a dusty leaded window overlooking the street below. Shelves filled with magical texts occupy the two remaining walls, and a threadbare rug of bright floral designs covers a dark wooden floor. An empty glass case sits next to the desk, a crack running down one side.

40064.name: Bedroom
40064.description: Curtains flutter against an open window, and intruding elements have across the room and atop a simple bureau. A bed sits to the side, brown stains upon the rotting sheets. The remains of a skeleton lie here, half hidden beneath the folds of mouldering cloth.

40184.name: Donation Room
40184.description: This room is bare save for a small carved wooden sign mounted upon the rear wall. Scraps of used and broken equipment lay along the walls and in the corners, untouched. Through the doorway to the south is the Inn's reception.

untouched.  Through the doorway to the south is the Inn's reception.

40401.name: The Mareldjan Orrery
40401.description: Darkly stained, heavy wooden shelves host a lifetime of notes and books attempting to discern the movement of the heavens. Dominating the center of the room, a large brass orrery rests upon a raised dais. Nearby the mechanical marvel is a desk covered in numerous calculations written in a delicate and tiny script.

40418.name: Magnificent Floating Platform
40418.description: You're not sure how this works, but the platform is floating above the Central Plaza of Mareldja. Looking over the edge, you can see the rooftops of the city and quite a ways past her gates. Shorebirds are perched on the edge, gawking and strutting around the perimeter. The platform is made up entirely out of polished bronze. In the center of the platform stands a flagpole waving the flag of Mareldja; a golden crown adorned with jewels floating above a body of water.

40433.name: Private Dining Room
40433.description: This quiet, private room is held away from the hustle and bustle of the lively tavern below. It is furnished with a long, polished wooden table that is surrounded by comfortable, upholstered chairs. The walls are adorned with simple, yet elegant tapestries and paintings that depict scenes of the city and its surrounding countryside. A fireplace at one end of the room provides a warm and cozy atmosphere, while a row of windows at the opposite end offers a view of the city below.
